headline: Public Health Laboratory Capacity Underfunded by RM180 Million
context: IMR and state public health laboratories face equipment shortages and staffing gaps affecting disease surveillance.

cards.big: Public health laboratories underfunded by RM180 million against operational requirements in 2025
cards.sub: Equipment maintenance backlogs and reagent shortages delay test results

cards.big: Average turnaround time for infectious disease confirmation increased from 3 to 7 days
cards.sub: Delayed diagnosis hampers outbreak response and increases transmission risk

cards.big: 38% of laboratory equipment at state health laboratories exceeds recommended service life
cards.sub: Aging equipment produces less reliable results and faces higher breakdown rates

cards.big: Taiwan's CDC laboratory network processes 95% of priority samples within 24 hours
cards.sub: Fully funded laboratory infrastructure is essential for effective disease surveillance

cards.big: Disease surveillance without functioning laboratories is like monitoring traffic without cameras
Should public health laboratory funding be protected from annual budget cut cycles
